intended_use: The Ahead 300 is indicated for use as an adjunct to standard clinical practice to aid in the evaluation of patients who are being considered for a head CT, who sustained a closed head injury within 72
intended_use_full: The Ahead 300 is indicated for use as an adjunct to standard clinical practice to aid in the evaluation of patients who are being considered for a head CT, who sustained a closed head injury within 72 hours, present with a Glasgow Coma Scale score (GCS) of 13-15, and are between the ages of 18-85 years. The Ahead 300 should not be used as a substitute for a CT scan. The device is intended to record, measure, analyze, and display brain electrical activity utilizing the calculation of standard quantitative EEG (QEEG) parameters from frontal locations on a patient's forehead.
target_condition: Closed head injury (traumatic brain injury) with evaluation for structural injury within 72 hours
ground_truth: Head CT scan (CT+ vs CT-) for structural brain injury
other_metrics: Co-primary endpoints of sensitivity and specificity successfully exceeded performance goals and demonstrated significantly improved performance over Ahead 200. High negative predictive value achieved. Repeatability and reproducibility of device results successfully demonstrated.
